PREVIOUSLY SENT REPORT WITH CORRECTED TIME AND UPDATED EF-1 RATING BASED ON STORM DAMAGE SURVEY. MONROE COUNTY SHERIFF DEPUTY CALLED TO REPORT NEIGHBORS OBSERVED A TORNADO WHICH RIPPED DOORS OFF HINGES...LOCATION WAS INDIAN MOUND TRAIL ON PLANTATION KEY...NEAR MILE MARKER 89.2 IN THE UPPER KEYS.

MONROE COUNTY SHERIFF DEPUTY CALLED TO REPORT NEIGHBORS OBSERVED A TORNADO WHICH RIPPED DOORS OFF HINGES...LOCATION WAS INDIAN MOUND TRAIL ON PLANTATION KEY...NEAR MILE MARKER 89.2 IN THE UPPER KEYS. INTENSITY UNKNOWN.

5 WATERSPOUTS WERE REPORTED ON THE GULF SIDE OF 33RD STREET IN MARATHON, BEGINNING AROUND 550 PM EDT. THE LONGEST-LIVED WATERSPOUT WAS OF MEDIUM WIDTH, WITH A CONDENSATION CLOUD THAT EXTENDED HALFWAY TO THE SURFACE, AND A CLEARLY VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THIS WATERSPOUT WAS ONGOING THROUGH 610 PM EDT. THE 4 ADDITIONAL WATERSPOUTS WERE VERY THIN WITH CONDENSATION CLOUDS THAT DID NOT EXTEND FAR FROM THE CLOUD BASE AND NO SPRAY RINGS WERE VISIBLE.
A CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN OBSERVED A SHORT-LIVED...NARROW WATERSPOUT NORTH OF KEY WEST.
PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT OFFSHORE GARRISON BIGHT KEY WEST VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

NEW INFORMATION ON GARRISON BIGHT WATERSPOUT. SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTOS RELAYED CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE TO 3 CATAMARANS AND TWISTING/DAMAGE TO DOCK AT A SAILING CENTER IN GARRISON BIGHT. 3 OTHER VESSELS AT THE SAME DOCK WITH MINOR DAMAGE WITH RIPPED COVERS AND TOPS. RELAYED EYEWITNESS REPORT OF WATERSPOUT TOUCHING DOWN ON TOP OF DOCKS AND BOATS AND VERY NARROW WITH SPRAY FROM SURFACE JUST A FEW FEET ACROSS. NO DAMAGE REPORTED ONSHORE. TIME ESTIMATED.
PUBLIC RELAYED A REPORT OF A WATERSPOUT JUST OFFSHORE HILTON HAVEN KEY WEST VIA NWS EMPLOYEE. THE WATERSPOUT CONSISTED OF A CONDENSATION FUNNEL ABOUT HALFWAY FROM CLOUD BASE TO HORIZON.
A MEMBER OF THE USCG AUXILLARY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF MARATHON, WITH EXTREME TILTING. DURATION UNKNOWN. CONFIRMATION OF SPRAY RING WAS NOT POSSIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTION OF VIEW.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A SKINNY AND STRAIGHT WATERSPOUT NORTH OF PLANTATION KEY. CONFIRMATION OF SPRAY RING WAS NOT POSSIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTION OF VIEW.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A SKINNY AND STRAIGHT WATERSPOUT A HALF MILE NE OF COTTRELL KEY. SPRAY RING VISIBILE. DURATION LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.

AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE, IN ADDITION TO SEVERAL MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC, REPORTED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS WEST OF KEY WEST. ONE OF THESE WATERSPOUTS HAD A SIGNIFICANT SPRAY RING, COUPLED WITH A THICK AND STRAIGHT FUNNEL. DURATION 10 MINUTES OR LESS.

SOCIAL MEDIA MINING INDICATE A PROBABLE WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF VACA KEY. CONDENSATION FUNNEL NOT FULLY INTACT. CONFIRMATION OF SPRAY RING WAS NOT POSSIBLE DUE TO OBSTRUCTION OF VIEW. TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 3 WATERSPOUTS OCEANSIDE OF KEY LARGO OFF MILE MARKER 106 OF US1. VIEW OF SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED BUT TWO EXTENDED MORE THAN THREE QUARTERS OF THE WAY BETWEEN THE CLOUD BASE AND HORIZON. THESE TWO WERE WIDE NEAR THE CLOUD BASE THEN GENERALLY PENCIL THIN TO THE HORIZON. THE THIRD WATERSPOUT WAS NOT AS WELL DEVELOPED.

TWO WATERSPOUTS WEST-NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST WERE OBSERVED FROM THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS FORECAST OFFICE ON WHITE STREET. THE CONDENSATION CLOUDS, ONE THIN AND ONE OF MEDIUM WIDTH, ONLY EXTENDED ABOUT A FIFTH OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. SPRAY RINGS WERE NOT OBSERVED AS VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE SOUTHWEST AROUND 5 KNOTS. DURATION OF BOTH WATERSPOUTS WAS APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES.

OVER THE TWO COURSE OF 25 MINUTES 4 WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED FROM THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS FORECAST OFFICE, ASSOCIATED WITH A SHOWER NORTH OF KEY WEST. ONE CONDENSATION CLOUD WAS MEDIUM WIDTH AND EXTENDED TO THE SURFACE WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THE REMAINING CONDENSATION CLOUDS WERE THIN AND EXTENDED ABOUT A FIFTH OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON WITH NO SPRAY RINGS VISIBLE AS VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE SOUTHWEST. ALL WATERSPOUTS HAD DISSIPATED BY 731 PM.
